title: Write a Dockerfile, Build a Docker Image, and Run a Docker Container

steps.title: Create a Dockerfile
steps.instruction: The first thing we need to do is create a docker file in the project route. Open your text editor and create a new file called docker file. with a capital D and no file extension

steps.title: Specify the Base Image
steps.instruction: The first instruction in a docker file is always FROM which specifies the base image. on the first line. This tells Docker to use the official Python 3.11 slim image as the starting point.

steps.title: Set the Working Directory
steps.instruction: On the next line type workdir This sets the working directory inside the container to slash app. All subsequent commands will run from this directory.

steps.title: Copy Requirements File
steps.instruction: Now type copy requirements.txt with a dot at the end. The dot means copy into the current working directory, which is slash app.

steps.title: Install Dependencies
steps.instruction: On the next line run pip install no cache dir requirements txt This installs the Python dependencies inside the container.

steps.title: Copy Application Code
steps.instruction: Now type copy dot dot to copy the rest of the application code into the container.

steps.title: Expose Port
steps.instruction: Then type expose5000 to tell Docker that the container listens on port 5000.

steps.title: Specify Command to Start Application
steps.instruction: Finally type cmd with square brackets, then the command to start the application. So cmd open square bracket quote python quote comma quote app dot pi quote close square bracket

steps.title: Build the Docker Image
steps.instruction: Now open your terminal and navigate to the project folder. Type docker build my flask app dot and press enter

steps.title: Run the Docker Container
steps.instruction: Type docker run dash p 5000 5000 my flask app and press enter. the "-P50005000 flag maps port 5000 on your host machine to port 5000 inside the container

steps.title: Verify the Application
steps.instruction: Open your browser and go to HTTP localhost 5000 You should see the message Hello from Docker in your browser.

steps.title: Stop the Container
steps.instruction: To stop the container press Ctrl-C in the terminal.
